overview: This four-color legendary midrange deck leverages Aether Revolt staples to control the board while applying steady pressure. By combining discard effects, sacrifice-based removal, and growing legendary threats, it aims to outvalue opponents in the mid-to-late game. The package thrives on card advantage and efficient board wipes disguised as creature sacrifices.
win_conditions: Outvalue opponents through card advantage from Crimson Chaos and Tinybones Joins Up
Close games with growing legendary threats like Tinybones Joins Up
Control the board with sacrifice-based removal and token generation

keep_criteria: Hands with early legendary creatures or token generators
Hands containing Crimson Chaos or Carnage with a creature to sacrifice
Mana bases that support four-color casting by turn three
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fixing) and a turn-1 or turn-2 play (e.g., a cheap legend, Glasspool Mimic, or spot removal).
Prioritize fixing over raw land count.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking removal or discard effects
Hands with only late-game legends and no early interaction
Hands missing two or more basic lands or key duals
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or hands consisting solely of high-cost legends and no answers.

early_game: Play lands and early legends to establish board presence
Use Crimson Chaos to disrupt opponent's hand while drawing cards
Hold creatures to fuel Carnage if needed
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-drop legend or cantrip if available.
Turn 2: Develop board with a 2-drop legend or Glasspool Mimic, using it to copy a threat or block.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetch/shock lands that can enter untapped or pair with basics for fixing.
Play around spot removal until you establish board presence.
mid_game: Sacrifice tokens or excess creatures to cast Carnage for targeted removal
Let Tinybones Joins Up grow while applying pressure
Maintain card advantage through discard and draw effects
Preserve mana for interaction until turn 3.
Use cantrips to dig for answers and maintain hand size.
Treat the graveyard as a secondary resource for recursion or value, avoiding unnecessary milling.
Balance removal, threats, and answers in hand
avoid flooding the board with unprotectable legends.
Commit threats when you have removal in hand or a board advantage.
late_game: Scale Tinybones Joins Up into a massive threat
Generate token armies with Glasspool Mimic deaths
Outlast opponents with superior card economy and board control
Tinybones Joins Up as a legendary commander/finisher, or a copied high-impact threat via Glasspool Mimic.
Carnage and Crimson Chaos serve as scalable finishers if they can resolve safely.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, removal for their answers, and they have no sweepers.
Commit when you can protect your finisher or have multiple threats that force unfavorable trades.

key_cards.name: Glasspool Mimic
key_cards.notes: Cast early to establish presence; its death triggers Thopter tokens that can fuel Carnage or block.

key_cards.name: Carnage
key_cards.notes: Requires a creature sacrifice; use tokens or excess legends to remove key threats efficiently.

key_cards.name: Crimson Chaos
key_cards.notes: Play on opponent's turn to discard their key cards, then draw to maintain tempo and find answers.

key_cards.name: Tinybones Joins Up
key_cards.notes: Enters with discard; grows larger with each creature you control, making it a primary win condition.

sequencing_tips: Hold creatures in hand to enable Carnage when facing big threats
Cast Crimson Chaos before playing creatures to maximize hand disruption
Save Glasspool Mimic deaths for when you need blockers or Carnage fuel
Prioritize keeping Tinybones Joins Up alive while building board state
Watch for legend rule interactions when copying or entering the battlefield.
Glasspool Mimic's tap ability is a key resource for value and defense
Tinybones Joins Up likely functions as a commander or legend enabler
use its ability to generate value or protect your board.
Common mistake: playing too many legends without protection or answers.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and use Carnage on their biggest threats. Keep discard in hand to slow their curve.
Apply steady pressure with legends and tokens. Use Crimson Chaos to strip their hand before they stabilize.
Race with growing threats while maintaining card advantage. Sacrifice tokens to remove their key pieces.

Use cheap threats, split casting costs, or force counters with cantrips. Protect key spells with counters or protection effects. Prioritize spells that are difficult to counter or have multiple targets.
Apply early interaction, discard, or artifact destruction. Do not let them stabilize; pressure early and disrupt their mana development. Use efficient removal to trade up against their large threats.

Win the resource war by prioritizing removal and card draw. Use Glasspool Mimic to copy their key threats or protect your own. Win the sideboard game by bringing in more interaction and legality tricks.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal, and combo pieces.
Note if the opponent is playing around specific legends.
Track graveyard activity for recursion threats.
Pay attention to mana development to predict their next move.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards emphasize discard, sacrifice removal, and scaling legends, indicating a midrange tempo approach.
